specific_location: Chico Basin Ranch, Upper Twin Pond, behind the dam there
description: The ratty looking bird had a brightly colored face pattern with orangy tones above and below the dark eye and a black tick behind a faint gray auricular patch. It had an orangy nape. The back with freshly molted adult feathers had dark centered feathers with light edgings creating a dark and white striped look. The tail was orangy, short and spiky. The breast was light in coloration with strong dark flank streaks. The bill was light colored and small for an Ammodramus sparrow. It was IDed to Ammodramus based on its relatively flat crown, but mostly its short spiky tail and secretive nature in a wet sedge meadow.Behaviors: The bird flushed out from in front of me while I was walking in a wet sedge meadow where it perched less than 1 foot above the sedges on a dead shrub. It moved later when I tried to approach too closely to the base of a Russian olive where it stayed partially hidden for a minute until it flew back into the sedge meadow where it dove in and disppeared.Call: none detectedPlumage: juvenile molting to adult
photographer: Photographer: bill maynard, bill maynard, bill maynard, bill maynard
